 {"id":"bd-1gqx","title":"Implement NetworkErrorDisplay for connection failures","description":"Create NetworkErrorDisplay in rch-common/src/ui/errors/network.rs specializing in connection errors:
- SSH connection failures with host resolution details
- Timeout errors with duration and retry information  
- Authentication failures with key/credential hints
- Port binding conflicts with process identification
- TLS/certificate errors with certificate details

Technical requirements:
- Parse underlying io::Error and ssh2 errors for detail extraction
- Display network path: local → daemon → worker
- Show which hop failed in multi-hop scenarios
- Include relevant environment variables (SSH_AUTH_SOCK, etc.)
- Suggest network diagnostic commands (ping, nc, ssh -vvv)
- Support --json for structured error output

Example for SSH failure:
╔═[ERROR]═══════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ ✗ RCH-E201: SSH Authentication Failed                 ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║ Worker 'build1' rejected SSH connection               ║
║                                                        ║
║ Connection path:                                       ║
║   local → rchd ✓ → build1 ✗                           ║
║                                                        ║
║ Details:                                               ║
║   Auth method: publickey                               ║
║   Key file: ~/.ssh/id_ed25519                         ║
║   Error: Permission denied (publickey)                ║
║                                                        ║
║ Suggestions:                                           ║
║   1. Check key is added: ssh-add -l                   ║
║   2. Verify authorized_keys on build1                 ║
║   3. Test manually: ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 build1   ║

 {"id":"bd-1gyl","title":"WS3.4: Unit tests for atomic field accessors","description":"Add unit tests for the new atomic accessors in WorkerState:

1. speed_score roundtrip:
   - set_speed_score(0.0), get_speed_score() == 0.0
   - set_speed_score(1.0), get_speed_score() == 1.0
   - set_speed_score(f64::MAX), get_speed_score() == f64::MAX
   - set_speed_score(f64::NAN) — verify NaN roundtrips (NaN != NaN but to_bits is stable)
   - set_speed_score(f64::INFINITY) — verify infinity roundtrips

2. last_latency_ms Option encoding:
   - Set None, get None
   - Set Some(100), get Some(100)
   - Set Some(0) — document behavior (0 is sentinel for None, so Some(0) would be lost)
     If this is a problem, use u64::MAX as sentinel instead and document

3. Concurrent access:
   - Spawn 10 tokio tasks that concurrently read/write speed_score
   - Verify no panics, no undefined behavior
   - Values may be stale (Relaxed ordering) but must be valid f64s

4. Verify accessor signature changes:
   - get_speed_score() is now sync (no longer async)
   - Callers in selection.rs compile without .await

Test location: rchd/src/workers.rs (inline #[cfg(test)] module)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:52:52.147883256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T22:53:10.694890768Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gyl","depends_on_id":"bd-2f5o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:52:52.154781290Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gyl","depends_on_id":"bd-3v9b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:53:08.944665497Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gyl","depends_on_id":"bd-4fgv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:53:10.694861374Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1gz8","title":"WS1.1: Convert TimingHistory::load to async","description":"Wrap TimingHistory::load() (line 881) in tokio::task::spawn_blocking:

Current code (line 886):
  match std::fs::read_to_string(&path) { ... }

Change callers (estimate_timing_for_build at line 983) to:
  let history = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(|| TimingHistory::load()).await.unwrap_or_default();

The load() method itself stays synchronous — we just move its execution off the async thread.

 {"id":"bd-1i8n","title":"Idea: Env var allowlist passthrough for remote exec","description":"## Background
Remote builds can diverge from local when environment variables (RUSTFLAGS, CARGO_TARGET_DIR, feature toggles) are not preserved. Today only implicit env is used on workers, which can cause subtle mismatches.

## Goals
- Provide an explicit allowlist of env vars to forward to workers.
- Safe-by-default: nothing sensitive forwarded unless allowed.
- Support global config and project override.

## Design / Approach
- Add config section: `environment.allowlist = ["RUSTFLAGS", "CARGO_TARGET_DIR", ...]`.
- Hook builds an env prefix for remote commands using current process env, filtered by allowlist.
- Include in rsync/SSH command wrapper; ensure quoting is safe.
- Provide `RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST` env override for quick usage.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Extend config schema + validation for allowlist entries.
- Add helper to extract allowed env vars and format for `sh -c` safely.
- Integrate into transfer pipeline (before wrap_command_with_toolchain/color).
- Update docs and `rch config show` output to surface allowlist.

## Tests
- Unit: allowlist parsing and filtering.
- Unit: safe quoting for env values with spaces/quotes.
- Integration: remote command sees forwarded env (mock worker).
- E2E: project config override works and is isolated per project.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Env passthrough is explicit and safe.
- No env leakage unless configured.
- Remote build parity improves for env-dependent builds.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-1i8n.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Env values with spaces/quotes/newlines must be safely quoted or rejected.
- Missing env vars in allowlist are ignored silently (no error noise).
- Logs must not print raw values for sensitive keys (redact).

## E2E Outline
- Allowlist + env set -> remote command sees value.
- Non-allowlisted env var not present remotely.
- Unsafe env value (newline) is blocked with warning.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs or new helper: env allowlist parsing + safe quoting.
- rch/src/config.rs: allowlist config merge/override.
- rch-common/src/ssh.rs: env prefix formatting for remote command.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-1i8n.sh: set env vars and verify remote sees allowlisted only.
- Ensure logs redact sensitive values (show key names only).

- Error code in header (RCH-Exxx format)

 {"id":"bd-mnaa","title":"Test: SSH Command Execution & Output Capture","description":"## Purpose
Test SSH command execution on real workers - running commands, capturing output, handling errors, and managing long-running processes.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_ssh_echo","phase":"execute","msg":"SSH command execution","data":{"cmd":"echo hello","worker":"css"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_ssh_echo","phase":"result","msg":"Command completed","data":{"exit_code":0,"stdout_bytes":6,"stderr_bytes":0,"duration_ms":45}}
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","test":"test_ssh_echo","phase":"output","msg":"Command output","data":{"stdout":"hello\\n","stderr":""}}
```

## Test Cases

### Basic Command Execution
1. Simple command:
   - Run: `echo 'hello world'`
   - Verify: stdout contains 'hello world'
   - Verify: exit code 0
   - Log: command, output, exit code

2. Command with exit code:
   - Run: `exit 42`
   - Verify: exit code 42 captured
   - Log: expected vs actual exit code

3. stderr output:
   - Run: `echo 'error' >&2`
   - Verify: stderr captured separately
   - Log: stderr content

### Environment & Working Directory
4. Working directory:
   - Run: `pwd` in specific directory
   - Verify: correct directory
   - Log: expected vs actual cwd

5. Environment variables:
   - Run: `echo $MY_VAR` with MY_VAR=test
   - Verify: env var passed correctly
   - Log: env vars set

### Long-Running & Complex Commands
6. Long output:
   - Run command producing >1MB output
   - Verify: all output captured
   - Log: total bytes, truncation status

7. Long-running command:
   - Run: `sleep 10` with timeout
   - Verify: timeout triggers correctly
   - Log: timeout value, actual duration

8. Binary output:
   - Run: `cat /bin/ls | head -c 1000`
   - Verify: binary data preserved
   - Log: binary bytes count

### Error Handling
9. Command not found:
   - Run: `nonexistent_command`
   - Verify: error captured, non-zero exit
   - Log: error type, exit code

10. Signal handling:
    - Kill long-running command
    - Verify: signal exit code (128+N)
    - Log: signal number, exit code

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands execute on real worker
- [ ] Output (stdout/stderr) correctly captured
- [ ] Exit codes correctly propagated
- [ ] Environment and cwd work correctly
- [ ] Timeouts and signals handled
- [ ] ALL phases logged with structured JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:16.045008068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:29:01.292444328Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:29:01.292184599Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-255k (SSH Infrastructure Tests).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mnaa","depends_on_id":"bd-255k","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-mnaa","depends_on_id":"bd-2bid","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-mnhp","title":"WS1.2: Convert TimingHistory::save to async","description":"Wrap TimingHistory::save() (line 893) in tokio::task::spawn_blocking:

Current code has three blocking ops:
  - Line 901: std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)
  - Line 906: std::fs::write(&temp_path, &content)
  - Line 907: std::fs::rename(temp_path, path)

Change caller (record_build_timing at line 948) to:
  let history_clone = history.clone();
  tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || history_clone.save());

The save() method stays synchronous. Its atomic temp-file-then-rename pattern is preserved.

+{"id":"bd-mnhp","title":"WS1.2: Convert TimingHistory::save to async","description":"Wrap TimingHistory::save() (line 893) in tokio::task::spawn_blocking:

Current code has three blocking ops:
  - Line 901: std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)
  - Line 906: std::fs::write(&temp_path, &content)
  - Line 907: std::fs::rename(temp_path, path)

Change caller (record_build_timing at line 948) to:
  let history_clone = history.clone();
  tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || history_clone.save());

The save() method stays synchronous. Its atomic temp-file-then-rename pattern is preserved.

 {"id":"bd-mosh","title":"Define error catalog with codes and remediation steps","description":"Create comprehensive error catalog in rch-common/src/errors/catalog.rs:
- Assign unique codes to all error types (RCH-E001 through RCH-E999)
- Group by category: E0xx (config), E1xx (network), E2xx (worker), E3xx (build), E4xx (transfer), E5xx (internal)
- Define remediation steps for each error
- Include documentation links/references
- Support i18n-ready message templates

Error categories and examples:
- RCH-E001: Invalid configuration file syntax
- RCH-E002: Missing required configuration field
- RCH-E101: Cannot connect to rchd daemon
- RCH-E102: Daemon connection timeout
- RCH-E201: Worker SSH authentication failed
- RCH-E202: Worker disk space exhausted
- RCH-E301: Compilation failed on worker
- RCH-E302: Build artifact not found
- RCH-E401: Workspace sync failed
- RCH-E501: Internal state corruption

Technical requirements:
- Implement as enum with associated data
- Derive Serialize/Deserialize for JSON output
- Include severity level (error, warning, fatal)
- Generate markdown documentation from catalog
- Unit tests for message formatting","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:01.908754185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T08:38:23.235514547Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T08:38:23.234729067Z","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive error catalog in rch-common/src/errors/catalog.rs with:
- 50+ error codes organized by category (E0xx config, E1xx network, E2xx worker, E3xx build, E4xx transfer, E5xx internal)
- Complete remediation steps for each error
- Documentation links per category

 {"id":"bd-mvpd","title":"EPIC: Progress Indicators and User Feedback","description":"# Progress Indicators and User Feedback

## Overview
RCH has good progress indicator infrastructure but doesn't use it consistently across all long-running operations. This creates uncertainty about whether operations are progressing or hung.

## Current State

### Strong Points
- Spinner and TransferProgress types exist in rch-common/src/ui/progress/
- Automatic TTY/JSON mode detection
- Unicode/ASCII fallback support

### Missing Progress Indicators
These operations provide no progress feedback:

1. **rch workers benchmark** - Runs speed tests on workers with no indication of progress
2. **rch self-test** - Compiles test project with no feedback during compilation
3. **rch workers sync-toolchain** - Runs rustup on workers, can take minutes with no feedback
4. **rch fleet deploy** - Deploys to multiple workers but shows no per-worker progress

### Missing Multi-Progress
When deploying to 5 workers simultaneously, users see nothing until all complete. Should show:
```
Deploying to fleet (3/5 complete)
  css ████████████████ 100% ✓
  csd ████████░░░░░░░░  50%
  cse ████░░░░░░░░░░░░  25%
  csf ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░   0% (connecting)
  csg ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░   0% (queued)
```

## Key Files
- rch-common/src/ui/progress/spinner.rs - Spinner implementation
- rch-common/src/ui/progress/transfer.rs - TransferProgress
- rch-common/src/ui/progress/compile.rs - CompilationProgress
- rch/src/commands.rs - Command implementations needing progress

## Success Criteria
- All operations >2s show progress indication
- Parallel operations show nested/grouped progress
- Progress works in both TTY and non-TTY modes
- Progress can be disabled with --quiet","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:59:01.704102251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T16:54:46.209492334Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mvsl","title":"Idea: Per-command priority hint for worker selection","description":"## Background
When many agents run concurrently, some commands are more urgent than others. A simple priority hint improves responsiveness for critical tasks.

## Goals
- Allow users/agents to tag a command with priority (low/normal/high).
- Feed priority into selection scoring and queue ordering.
- Maintain fairness; avoid starvation.

## Design / Approach
- Use env var `RCH_PRIORITY=low|normal|high` or CLI flag in `rch compile`.
- Extend SelectionRequest with priority field; adjust selection weights or queue priority.
- Log priority decisions in daemon events.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Extend protocol types and daemon selection logic.
- Add CLI/env plumbing in hook and compile command.
- Add documentation and examples.

## Tests
- Unit: priority parsing + default.
- Integration: high priority selects from faster workers when available.
- E2E: priority logged and visible in status output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Priority hint affects selection without breaking fairness.
- Default behavior unchanged when priority not set.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-mvsl.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Invalid priority -> default to normal with warning.
- Prevent starvation (priority affects selection, not infinite queueing).
- Priority visible in status/diagnostics.

## E2E Outline
- RCH_PRIORITY=high prefers fastest worker.
- RCH_PRIORITY=low avoids fastest when others available.
- Logs show priority value used.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch-common/src/types.rs: priority parsing + default.
- rchd/src/selection.rs: priority influence on scoring.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-mvsl.sh: RCH_PRIORITY=high prefers fastest worker.
